Off Calendar Jun-13-2016 Continued To Aug-10-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-25-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Yesenia Perez of Foley & Lardner LLP telephone number 415-434-4484 for Cross-Defendants Douglas Bennett and Hudson River Partners 1, Inc.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANTS DOUGLAS BENNETT and HUDSON RIVER PARTNERS 1, INC.'S DEMURRER to all three claims alleged in the cross-complaint filed by Hudson River Partners I, LLP is sustained as to all three claims with twenty days leave to amend. As pled, all three claims accrued in 2008 and cross-complainant did not specifically allege that cross-defendants are equitably estopped to assert that the claims are time-barred. Because it appears that there is a reasonable possibility that cross-complainant can allege in good faith that cross-defendants are equitably estopped to assert the statute of limitations, cross-complainant is given leave to do so. (See Sofranek v. Merced County (2007) 146 Cal. App. 4th 1238 (reversing grant of demurrer on the grounds that plaintiff's claims were time-barred because the plaintiff sufficiently alleged estoppel to assert the statute of limitations)). Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-25-2016 Continued To Jun-29-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-21-2016 Continued To May-11-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-10-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. YESENIA GARCIA PEREZ, ESQ. of Foley & Lardner LLP, Tel No. 415-434-4484 appearing for the Plaintiff;
No other appearances made in this matter.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT HUDSON RIVER PARTNERS I, L.P.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled as to both causes of action in the first amended complaint. Liberally construed, the first amended complaint alleges sufficient facts to invoke the discovery rule to avoid the bar of the statute of limitations. Mr. Bennett alleges that: 1) he reasonably relied on the oral and written assurances of Mr. Malek that the release would be "corrected"; and 2) he had no reason to believe that Mr. Malek's assurances were inaccurate until he contacted Mr. Malek relating to the loan in 2015. Mr. Bennett's interpretation of the parties' agreements must be accepted as true on a demurrer unless the agreements are not reasonably susceptible to that interpretation. Mr. Bennett's interpretation is permissible under this standard. The second claim is not duplicative of the first claim. The first claim seeks reformation of the release and the second claim seeks a determination of the parties' rights and liabilities in their agreements. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/HK)
